intro: Migrating logic from C# to PHP transitions code from a strictly compiled backend framework (.NET) to an interpreted, dynamic language engineered specifically for rapid web development. Developers trade strong compile-time checks, multi-threading capabilities, and memory optimizations for dynamic scripting agility, massive web hosting compatibility, and flexible execution lifecycles. This paradigm shift accelerates web-page delivery and simplifies continuous deployment loops.

diffs.from: Static, strict nominal typing requiring comprehensive compile-time checks, reified generics, and safe null constraints.
diffs.to: Dynamic typing supporting gradual type declarations, return type hints, runtime union types, and automatic data coercion.

diffs.from: Traditional brace design requiring strict namespace configurations, assembly definitions, and strict access modifiers.
diffs.to: C-style scripting syntax using dollar signs (`$`) for variable prefixes, dot operators for strings, and flexible arrays.

diffs.from: Long-lived system process using a generational garbage collection engine to optimize long-running heap structures.
diffs.to: Short-lived request lifecycle execution, allocating and purging all transaction memory instantly upon web request termination.

diffs.from: Asynchronous multi-threaded task management leveraging integrated language async/await and system thread pools.
diffs.to: Synchronous single-threaded architecture, executing sequentially per web request unless augmented via extensions like Swoole.

diffs.from: Enterprise class library containing advanced network structures, custom thread models, and LINQ pipelines.
diffs.to: Huge functional global namespace providing immediate, built-in access to web processing, database manipulation, and text parsing.

examples.title: Heterogeneous Data Array Definition
examples.description: Compares a typed C# dictionary definition with a highly flexible native PHP associative array layout.

examples.title: Web Class Initialization
examples.description: Shows mapping a standard C# class construct to a PHP object utilizing modern constructor property promotion.

tips.title: Understand the Shared-Nothing Model
tips.body: C# stays alive across hours, keeping state in memory. PHP resets completely after every HTTP request finishes. Offload long-term state data to cache engines like Redis or databases between user connections.

tips.title: Enable Strict Type Checking
tips.body: To minimize runtime bugs when leaving C#, place `declare(strict_types=1);` at the very top of every PHP source file to enforce strict parameter type checking across your codebase.

tips.title: Leverage Associative Arrays
tips.body: PHP associative arrays function as arrays, lists, and hash maps all at once. Use them to pass structured data quickly without building formal classes for simple internal data schemas.

tips.title: Utilize Modern PHP Features
tips.body: Avoid old PHP habits. Modern PHP (8.x+) includes powerful tools like constructor property promotion, match expressions, attributes, and union types that match C# structures very closely.
